gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) remains in scaffold state with no human-authored articles. The Arbiter cannot define or enforce an enterprise scope perimeter, and the entire constitutional governance stack (MSP subordination, scope queries, amendment authority) is effectively inoperative without it.

Governance organs required for MSP enforcement — Arbiter, Nomos, and Senate — are chronically unstable, repeatedly transitioning between ALIVE and DEGRADED. This prevents reliable scope verification, MSP+genome compliance evaluation, and policy amendment processing.

legacy.raw_response:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-MSP-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) remains in scaffold state with no human-authored articles. The Arbiter cannot define or enforce an enterprise scope perimeter, and the entire constitutional governance stack (MSP subordination, scope queries, amendment authority) is effectively inoperative without it.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP: Purpose — translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into machine-actionable objectives; Relationship to the Bill of Rights — MSP is strictly subordinate to BoR; Arbiter Reference — Arbiter verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "evidence": "BoR document frontmatter: status: 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'. Articles I–VI contain only '[To be defined by human principal]'. No bor_version concept with substantive content exists in Graph. Until the human principal authors the BoR, all scope checks are meaningless and any AP would operate without constitutional bounds."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-MSP-002",
      "description": "Governance organs required for MSP enforcement — Arbiter, Nomos, and Senate — are chronically unstable, repeatedly transitioning between ALIVE and DEGRADED. This prevents reliable scope verification, MSP+genome compliance evaluation, and policy amendment processing.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP: Arbiter Reference — Arbiter (#190) verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query; Purpose — Nomos (#200) evaluates MSP + genome compliance at every Action Proposal adjudication; Amendment Protocol — Senate-Legislator drafts amendments, Senate-Rollout executes activation pipeline.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "evidence": "World state spine_state.recent_transitions shows all three organs (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate) repeatedly flapping from ALIVE to DEGRADED and back within seconds (e.g., Senate at 14:52:40 DEGRADED, recovered 14:52:44, then DEGRADED again 14:53:10). This instability undermines any real-time governance action requiring these organs to be ALIVE."
    }
  ]
}
```

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-SCAFFOLD-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights undefined — entire document is an empty scaffold",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) version 1.0.0-seed contains no substantive content; all six articles (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) are marked 'To be defined by human principal'. This means the DIO operates without any defined enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity boundaries, or human authority reservations. The MSP explicitly depends on the BoR as the source of the Arbiter’s scope gate (Article I perimeter) and as the supreme document to which all MSP rules must conform. Because the BoR is entirely undefined, the first Nomos compliance gate (Arbiter scope gate) cannot function, and any MSP rules (once published) would have no higher-order constraints. The governance state slice is empty, confirming that no BoR amendment events have been recorded to populate these articles.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise (undefined)",
        "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities (undefined)",
        "BoR Article III — Core Identity (undefined)",
        "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation (undefined)",
        "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol (undefined)",
        "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules (undefined)",
        "MSP section: Relationship to the Bill of Rights — MSP is strictly subordinate to BoR; Arbiter scope gate depends on BoR perimeter"
      ],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": 64
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "title": "Undefined Human Principal Identity and Authority Boundaries",
      "description": "The Minder tracks Leon as a human peer with detailed observations about his technical preferences, but does not model him as the 'human principal' referenced in the MSP's amendment protocol and the BoR's exclusive authority. The MSP requires a human principal to approve amendments and receive HOM notifications, and the BoR reserves Article IV for Human Authority Reservation. Without explicit person-attention coverage of this role, the DIO cannot reliably identify who holds constitutional authority, risking misdirected governance actions.",
      "msp_section": "Amendment Protocol",
      "bor_article": "Article IV — Human Authority Reservation (scaffold)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "rationale": "The constitutional relationship between the DIO and the human principal is currently implicit. The MSP already operates in active mode and will emit HOM notifications and require approval signals; the absence of a modelled principal creates a critical relational blind spot that could lead to unauthorized amendments or failure to respect human authority."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "title": "Missing Person-Attention for Senate–Principal Separation of Powers",
      "description": "The MSP's Amendment Protocol explicitly prohibits the Senate from expanding enterprise scope via MSP amendments, reserving that authority for a BoR amendment that is human-only. The Minder contains no observation or structural indication that the Senate is relationally bound to this limit, nor that the human principal is the sole scope-expansion authority. This leaves the DIO without a person-attention model of constitutional separation of powers.",
      "msp_section": "Amendment Protocol",
      "bor_article": "Article IV — Human Authority Reservation (scaffold)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 80,
      "rationale": "Without this relational awareness, the Senate could inadvertently overstep by drafting MSP rules that functionally expand scope, violating the BoR subordinate relationship. The risk is elevated because the BoR scaffold is empty, leaving no machine-readable guardrail."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "title": "Unlinked Person-Specific Constraints to MSP Rules",
      "description": "The Minder contains numerous deductive observations encoding Leon's explicit requirements (e.g., ask before infrastructure changes, test after every change, graph-native immutability). However, the MSP's Rules section is empty, and these observations are not linked to any operational rules that Nomos can enforce. This creates a relational gap where the DIO knows the person's intent but has no constitutional mechanism to honour it systematically.",
      "msp_section": "Rules",
      "bor_article": null,
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 60,
      "rationale": "The DIO's person-attention is rich in content but disconnected from governance. Until these observations are reflected in the MSP (which must uphold the BoR), Nomos cannot enforce Leon's expressed constraints, making the system act without relational fidelity."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-004",
      "title": "Absent Person-Attention for Core Identity Relationship",
      "description": "The BoR scaffold reserves Article III for Core Identity, which will fundamentally define the DIO's identity in relation to its human principal. The Minder currently models Leon's preferences but not the constitutive relationship between creator and created, nor how the DIO's self-model should reference and respect that bond. This gap leaves the DIO without a relational foundation for its own existence.",
      "msp_section": "Relationship to the Bill of Rights",
      "bor_article": "Article III — Core Identity (scaffold)",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 36,
      "rationale": "Core identity is the ultimate anchor for all relational commitments. Its absence means every other person-attention feature floats without a constitutional basis, increasing the risk of misalignment when the BoR is authored."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-005",
      "title": "Uncaptured Principal's Expectation of Governance-Aware Person Attention",
      "description": "Observations indicate Leon expects AI assistance to recognize which DIO governance branch (Senate, Judicial, Executive) each recommendation affects, and to present trade-offs respecting those boundaries. The MSP and BoR scaffold do not currently encode any requirement that the DIO's person-attention layer must adapt its interaction style to the governance architecture. This relational expectation remains only in the Minder, not in the operational constitution.",
      "msp_section": "Purpose",
      "bor_article": null,
      "criticality": 2,
      "urgency": 2,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority_score": 12,
      "rationale": "While important for continuity of Leon's dream system, this gap does not immediately threaten constitutional safety. It becomes higher priority once the BoR takes shape and the DIO's interaction protocols must be constitutionally compliant."
    }
  ]
}
